instruction: Extract the following three facts from the passage and return them as a JSON object with keys: drug_name, mechanism_of_action, primary_indication. Do not include any other keys or text outside the JSON object.
context: Pembrolizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that works by blocking the programmed death-1 (PD-1) receptor on T-cells, thereby preventing tumor cells from evading immune detection. It is primarily indicated for the treatment of unresectable or metastatic melanoma, though its approved indications have expanded to include non-small cell lung cancer, head and neck squamous cell carcinoma, and several other solid tumors. Pembrolizumab is administered intravenously every three weeks at a dose of 200 mg.

response.text: {"drug_name": "pembrolizumab", "mechanism_of_action": "blocks the PD-1 receptor on T-cells, preventing tumor cells from evading immune detection", "primary_indication": "unresectable or metastatic melanoma"}

instruction: Extract all party names and their roles from the contract excerpt below. Return a bullet list where each line is: '- [Party Name]: [Role]'. Do not include dates, amounts, or any other information. If a party appears multiple times under different names, list the canonical name and note the alias in parentheses.
context: This Software License Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of January 15, 2024, by and between Nexus Technology Solutions, Inc., a Delaware corporation ("Licensor"), and Brightwave Digital Partners LLC, a California limited liability company ("Licensee"). The Agreement is guaranteed by Marcus T. Holloway ("Guarantor"), the sole managing member of Brightwave Digital Partners LLC. Nexus Technology Solutions, Inc. may also be referred to herein as "NTS" for the purposes of brevity.

response.text:
- Nexus Technology Solutions, Inc. (also "NTS"): Licensor
- Brightwave Digital Partners LLC: Licensee
- Marcus T. Holloway: Guarantor

instruction: From the news article excerpt below, extract the following facts and return them as a JSON object: {"event": "", "location": "", "casualties": {"dead": <int or null>, "injured": <int or null>}, "responsible_party": <string or null>}. Use null for any field not explicitly stated in the text. Do not infer or estimate.
context: A 6.8-magnitude earthquake struck the coastal city of Antakya, Turkey on Tuesday evening, collapsing at least 14 buildings and leaving an estimated 312 people injured according to the Turkish Disaster and Emergency Management Authority (AFAD). As of Wednesday morning, rescue teams had recovered 23 bodies from the rubble. The earthquake is a natural disaster; no responsible party applies. Tremors were felt as far away as Beirut and Cyprus.

instruction: Extract every configuration parameter mentioned in the technical documentation snippet below. Return a bullet list. Each bullet must follow the format: '- `<param_name>` (type: <type>, default: <default or "none">): <one-sentence description>'. Do not include parameters that are mentioned but have no described purpose.
context: The connection pool is configured via the following settings. `pool_size` (integer, default: 10) sets the maximum number of persistent connections maintained by the pool. `pool_timeout` (float, default: 30.0) specifies how many seconds to wait for a connection to become available before raising a TimeoutError. `pool_recycle` (integer, default: -1) controls how many seconds a connection may be reused before it is replaced; -1 disables recycling. `pool_pre_ping` (boolean, default: False) enables a connectivity check before each connection is returned from the pool, discarding stale connections automatically. An optional label `pool_logging_name` may be provided for diagnostic purposes but has no effect on behavior.

response.text:
- `pool_size` (type: integer, default: 10): Sets the maximum number of persistent connections maintained by the pool.
- `pool_timeout` (type: float, default: 30.0): Specifies how many seconds to wait for a connection before raising a TimeoutError.
- `pool_recycle` (type: integer, default: -1): Controls how many seconds a connection may be reused before replacement; -1 disables recycling.
- `pool_pre_ping` (type: boolean, default: False): Enables a connectivity check before returning a connection from the pool, discarding stale connections.

instruction: From the earnings call excerpt, extract the three financial metrics explicitly stated with numeric values. Return a JSON array where each element is: {"metric": "", "value": "", "period": ""}. Use the exact wording from the text for metric names. Do not calculate or derive values not stated explicitly.
context: Good morning everyone. I'm pleased to report that for Q3 fiscal year 2024, total revenue came in at $4.7 billion, representing 12% year-over-year growth. Gross margin expanded to 61.3%, up from 58.9% in the prior year quarter. We also saw strong operational leverage, with operating income reaching $820 million, a 19% increase compared to Q3 FY2023. We remain comfortable with our full-year guidance and look forward to providing further detail during Q&A.

response.text: [{"metric": "total revenue", "value": "$4.7 billion", "period": "Q3 fiscal year 2024"}, {"metric": "Gross margin", "value": "61.3%", "period": "Q3 fiscal year 2024"}, {"metric": "operating income", "value": "$820 million", "period": "Q3 fiscal year 2024"}]
